mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is ideal for teams looking to enhance their security posture within Kubernetes environments. Key trade-offs include the need for additional resource allocation and potential performance impacts during vulnerability scanning.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official Aqua Security Helm charts repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with version 0.23.0.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the 'trivy-system' namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration resource to define how the Trivy Operator should scan your resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/nginx
    tag: latest
EOF

```

> Creates a TrivyConfiguration for scanning the nginx image.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/nginx
    tag: latest
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that any vulnerability data collected will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfig-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up existing TrivyConfiguration resources.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to version 0.23.0.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfig-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivy-operator pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create TrivyConfiguration'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyConfiguration resource may have invalid specifications.

**Fix:**
Review the YAML definition for errors and ensure all required fields are specified correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No valid image found for scanning'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image in the TrivyConfiguration may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Ensure the image repository and tag are correct and accessible from the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm install fails with 'chart not found''
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified version of the Helm chart may not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Verify the version of the chart using 'helm search repo aqua/trivy-operator' and adjust the version accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image scanning configurations and improved error handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system'.

    tag: latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
